reasoning: The statistical trend analysis from yfinance provided a combined estimate of P(increase) = 0.61 based on linear trend and historical seasonality. Weekly stock movements are typically noisy and largely unpredictable, meaning any prediction should stay close to 0.5 unless there is an overwhelming signal. Considering the statistical model's positive bias (0.61) weighed against the pronounced negative short-term momentum, I'm adjusting slightly above a coin-flip to 0.54. This reflects a modest expectation of stability or mean reversion following an extended drop, while heavily leaning toward the base rate of ~0.5.
